summary: Implemented FileLockManager with read-write locks and deadlock prevention. Created LockToken frozen dataclass and FileLockManager class with acquire_read/write/multiple methods, release methods, and automatic lock cleanup thread. All paths are normalized via os.path.abspath(). Deadlock prevention achieved through atomic multi-file acquisition in sorted order. Comprehensive test suite with 45 unit tests covering all lock scenarios, thread safety, and edge cases. All code passes mypy --strict.
test_summary: 45 passed in 18.27s. All required acceptance criteria tests pass: test_exclusive_write_blocks_read, test_multiple_read_locks_allowed, test_lock_timeout_auto_release, test_acquire_multiple_sorted_order. Tests include unit tests for lock acquisition/release, thread safety tests with concurrent agents, timeout tests with mocked time, path normalization tests, and edge case handling.
codebase_learnings: Threading model: Uses RLock (re-entrant lock) for internal state protection to allow cleanup thread to operate safely. Event-based waiting with condition variable pattern for waiting agents.
Path normalization critical: All paths go through os.path.abspath() to ensure consistent file identity across relative/absolute path variations.

Deadlock prevention by design: acquire_multiple() sorts all paths alphabetically before acquisition, preventing circular wait dependencies. Single-file methods (acquire_read/write) also work correctly.
Type annotations: Used collections.deque instead of custom implementation. Generic type handling requires proper imports (Deque, Tuple from typing).
Test patterns: Use threading.Thread for concurrent scenarios, monkeypatch + unittest.mock.patch for time mocking in timeout tests. Thread exceptions in pytest show as warnings but don't fail tests.

Lock timeout semantics: separate timeouts per lock type, handled by detecting elapsed time and remaining timeout in loop. All-or-nothing semantics in acquire_multiple require explicit cleanup on partial failure.
File naming: Tests use descriptive class-based organization (TestAcquireWrite, TestConcurrency) rather than function-based, improving readability and test discovery.

agent_retro.what_worked_well: Architecture document provided precise interface specifications that translated directly to implementation. Test requirements were clear and comprehensive. Threading patterns were straightforward with proper locking discipline. Type checking with mypy caught an error early (generic type annotation for deque).
agent_retro.challenges: The waiting queue pattern with events required careful lock management to avoid deadlocks between the manager thread and waiting threads. Timeout handling with multiple acquire calls in acquire_multiple required elapsed time tracking across iterations.
agent_retro.if_doing_again: Consider using collections.abc.deque upfront rather than custom implementation. The RLock choice was correct but took some thought to ensure re-entrancy was needed (cleanup thread needs to call release while possibly holding locks). Thread notification pattern (setting events) is sound but requires comments to explain the non-blocking release logic.
agent_retro.estimated_effort: Implementation took ~2 hours including typing, testing, and debugging. Could have been 20% faster with direct deque import upfront. Test suite was straightforward to write given the clear acceptance criteria.
